diagrams.title: OTP Encryption/Decryption Process
diagrams.description: Shows the complete flow of OTP encryption using XOR operation, demonstrating that encryption and decryption are the same operation due to XOR's self-inverse property.
diagrams.purpose: Visualize the symmetric nature of OTP where same XOR operation serves both encryption and decryption, reinforcing the mathematical foundation of perfect secrecy.

diagrams.title: Key Reuse Attack on OTP
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the catastrophic security failure when OTP key is reused, showing how XORing two ciphertexts eliminates the key and reveals plaintext relationship.
elements: XOR operation between C1 and C2
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate why key reuse completely breaks OTP security and emphasizes the critical requirement of using each key exactly once.
